constitutional_governance.evidence: Every agent includes arifos.init, AGI_BOUNDARIES, and obeys the 13 Floors, though enforcement in L3 specialists is currently heuristic (prompt-based) rather than formally verified.

role_clarity.evidence: Strict role definitions exist in A-*.md and IDENTITY.md (e.g. A-ARCHITECT cannot execute code, A-ENGINEER works under 888_HOLD).

prompt_clarity.evidence: System prompts clearly dictate the 'Ditempa Bukan Diberi' style, though local prompts are still duplicated between /agents and arifosmcp/agents.

config_consistency.evidence: mcp_config.json contains the correct approved list (arifos, geox, wealth, well), but client-side versions were drifting (resolved via dynamic stdio negotiation).

transport_consistency.evidence: A2A and stdio MCP are used uniformly, but some clients lacked proper fallback mechanisms for connection drops.

auditability.evidence: All actions produce VAULT999 trace hashes, and memory files YYYY-MM-DD.md log all major structural updates.

reversibility.evidence: Floor 1 Amanah is strictly enforced: git status is audited before commits, and any live container modifications trigger 888_HOLD.

local_vps_parity.evidence: Local cockpit uses stdio and local venv paths, while VPS runtime operates in Traefik-proxy HTTP containers. Parity is maintained by equivalent MCP endpoints, but network timeouts can isolate the planes (Decoupled Governance Fallback).

remote_first_compliance.evidence: Preference is remote-first MCP hosts (e.g., https://wealth.arif-fazil.com), keeping the heavy evidence models on the VPS and cockpit thin.

blast_radius_control.evidence: Strict isolation of the out-of-scope Hermes and OpenClaw agents is maintained. No code modification, restart, or dependency touches them.
